pattern: Backtracking over every position inserts one operator at a time, tracking the running value and the last multiplicative term to correctly handle operator precedence without re-evaluating from scratch.
hints: At each step choose a substring of remaining digits (no leading zeros for multi-digit numbers) as the next operand, then pick an operator to connect it.

Carry two accumulators: the total running value and the value of the most recent multiplicative segment, because a future multiplication must undo the additive contribution of that segment and replace it.
Only record an expression when all digits are consumed and the running value equals the target.

edge_inputs.description: two expressions yield target 8 with multiplication
leading zero must not form multi-digit operand

stress.description: 8-digit string with moderate target to stress backtracking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(1, size), 8)
    digits = ''.join(str(rng.randint(1, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    target = rng.randint(-100, 100)
    return [digits, target]
stress.note: cap at 8 digits; 10 digits with all single-digit operands gives 4^9 paths
